title: Popover
description: Foldkit-native shadcn base-nova Popover wrapper composed from the local Base UI Popover primitive, shadcn slot classes, local class canonicalization, deterministic modal and placement attributes, and local static examples.

examples.title: PopoverBasic
examples.description: Popover with header, title, description, and start alignment.

examples.title: PopoverDemo
examples.description: Dimensions popover with local static field and input markup.

examples.title: PopoverForm
examples.description: Compact form popover using local static Field and Label shells.

examples.title: PopoverAlignments
examples.description: Three deterministic alignments rendered from the local Popover wrapper.

examples.title: PopoverRtl
examples.description: RTL popover examples with physical and logical side attributes.

deviations.summary: The shadcn wrapper composes local Popover attributes instead of importing upstream Base UI React primitives.
deviations.rationale: Installable Foldkit source must not import React or upstream Base UI runtime packages.

deviations.summary: Examples use local static Button, Field, Label, Input, and RTL shells instead of unavailable origin app dependencies.
deviations.rationale: Field and Label are outside this component slice. Static local markup preserves the relevant Popover DOM, class, ARIA, and visual contracts.

deviations.summary: Positioner and content expose side, align, offset, and collision attributes without runtime DOM measurement or collision flipping.
deviations.rationale: Measured collision behavior belongs behind a shared measurement service, not inside the shadcn render helper.

deviations.summary: The wrapper passes through the local Base UI modal surface, including data and ARIA attributes, but inherits the partial non-dialog trap-focus and pointer-isolation support.
deviations.rationale: The local Base UI Popover exposes modal as structured Foldkit data and commands without claiming full upstream modal parity where Foldkit has no reusable non-dialog focus-trap or pointer-isolation primitive.
